Directive.name: abort
Directive.md_description: This directive stops the assembly immediately.

Directive.name: abort (coff)
Directive.md_description: When producing COFF output, `as` accepts this directive as a synonym for '`.abort`'.

Directive.name: align
Directive.md_description: Pad the location counter (in the current subsection) to a particular storage boundary.

Directive.name: altmacro
Directive.md_description: Enable alternate macro mode.

Directive.name: ascii
Directive.md_description: `.ascii` expects zero or more string literals (see [Strings](https://sourceware.org/binutils/docs-2.41/as/Strings.html)) separated by commas. It assembles each string (with no automatic trailing zero byte) into consecutive addresses.

Directive.name: asciz
Directive.md_description: `.asciz` is just like `.ascii`, but each string is followed by a zero byte. The "z" in '`.asciz`' stands for "zero".

Directive.name: attach_to_group
Directive.md_description: Attaches the current section to the named group.

Directive.name: balign
Directive.md_description: Pad the location counter (in the current subsection) to a particular storage boundary.

Directive.name: bss
Directive.md_description: `.bss` tells `as` to assemble the following statements onto the end of the bss section.

Directive.name: bundle_align_mode
Directive.md_description: `.bundle_align_mode` enables or disables *aligned instruction bundle* mode. In this mode, sequences of adjacent instructions are grouped into fixed-sized *bundles*.

Directive.name: bundle_lock
Directive.md_description: The `.bundle_lock` and directive `.bundle_unlock` directives allow explicit control over instruction bundle padding.

Directive.name: bundle_unlock
Directive.md_description: The `.bundle_lock` and directive `.bundle_unlock` directives allow explicit control over instruction bundle padding.

Directive.name: byte
Directive.md_description: `.byte` expects zero or more expressions, separated by commas. Each expression is assembled into the next byte.

Directive.name: cfi_sections
Directive.md_description: `.cfi_sections` may be used to specify whether CFI directives should emit `.eh_frame` section, `.debug_frame` section and/or `.sframe` section.

Directive.name: cfi_startproc
Directive.md_description: `.cfi_startproc` is used at the beginning of each function that should have an entry in `.eh_frame`. It initializes some internal data structures. Don't forget to close the function by `.cfi_endproc`.

Directive.name: cfi_endproc
Directive.md_description: `.cfi_endproc` is used at the end of a function where it closes its unwind entry previously opened by `.cfi_startproc`, and emits it to `.eh_frame`.

Directive.name: cfi_personality
Directive.md_description: `.cfi_personality` defines personality routine and its encoding.

Directive.name: cfi_personality_id
Directive.md_description: `.cfi_personality_id` defines a personality routine by its index as defined in a compact unwinding format.

Directive.name: cfi_fde_data
Directive.md_description: `.cfi_fde_data` is used to describe the compact unwind opcodes to be used for the current function.

Directive.name: cfi_lsda
Directive.md_description: `.cfi_lsda` defines LSDA and its encoding.

Directive.name: cfi_inline_lsda
Directive.md_description: `.cfi_inline_lsda` marks the start of a LSDA data section and switches to the corresponding `.gnu.extab` section.

Directive.name: cfi_def_cfa
Directive.md_description: `.cfi_def_cfa` defines a rule for computing CFA as: *take address from register and add offset to it*.

Directive.name: cfi_def_cfa_register
Directive.md_description: `.cfi_def_cfa_register` modifies a rule for computing CFA. From now on register will be used instead of the old one. Offset remains the same.

Directive.name: cfi_def_cfa_offset
Directive.md_description: `.cfi_def_cfa_offset` modifies a rule for computing CFA. Register remains the same, but offset is new.

Directive.name: cfi_adjust_cfa_offset
Directive.md_description: Same as `.cfi_def_cfa_offset` but *offset* is a relative value that is added/subtracted from the previous offset.

Directive.name: cfi_offset
Directive.md_description: Previous value of *register* is saved at offset *offset* from CFA.

Directive.name: cfi_val_offset
Directive.md_description: Previous value of register is CFA + *offset*.

Directive.name: cfi_rel_offset
Directive.md_description: Previous value of *register* is saved at offset *offset* from the current CFA register.

Directive.name: cfi_register
Directive.md_description: Previous value of *register1* is saved in register *register2*.

Directive.name: cfi_restore
Directive.md_description: `.cfi_restore` says that the rule for *register* is now the same as it was at the beginning of the function, after all initial instruction added by `.cfi_startproc` were executed.

Directive.name: cfi_undefined
Directive.md_description: From now on the previous value of *register* can't be restored anymore.

Directive.name: cfi_same_value
Directive.md_description: Current value of *register* is the same like in the previous frame, i.e. no restoration needed.

Directive.name: cfi_remember_state
Directive.md_description: `.cfi_remember_state` pushes the set of rules for every register onto an implicit stack.

Directive.name: cfi_restore_state
Directive.md_description: `.cfi_restore_state` pops the set of rules for every register off the stack and places them in the current row.

Directive.name: cfi_return_column
Directive.md_description: Change return column *register*, i.e. the return address is either directly in *register* or can be accessed by rules for *register*.

Directive.name: cfi_signal_frame
Directive.md_description: Mark current function as signal trampoline.

Directive.name: cfi_window_save
Directive.md_description: SPARC register window has been saved.

Directive.name: cfi_escape
Directive.md_description: Allows the user to add arbitrary bytes to the unwind info. One might use this to add OS-specific CFI opcodes, or generic CFI opcodes that GAS does not yet support.

Directive.name: cfi_val_encoded_addr
Directive.md_description: The current value of *register* is *label*. The value of *label* will be encoded in the output file according to *encoding*; see the description of `.cfi_personality` for details on this encoding.
